displayTitle: Four Words That Defend the Verdict
kicker: PT LEVEL II // EVALUATION OF INDICATIONS

beats.narration: Under the lamp, every glowing mark is an indication. Level One records what appears. Level Two decides what it means — and signs for it.

payload.title: FOUR WORDS, ONE VERDICT
payload.panelLabel: PT // THE FLAW YOU ANSWER FOR

beats.narration: Sort with four words. An indication is true or false. True ones split again: nonrelevant traces the part's design, relevant comes from a discontinuity.
The tells separate them. False wipes away and never redevelops. Nonrelevant is uniform, matching the drawing. Relevant is irregular, where no feature lives.

payload.heading: Read the tell, not the glow

beats.narration: Only relevant indications get evaluated. Classify linear or rounded, measure the indication itself, compare to the governing acceptance criteria — then disposition the part.

payload.heading: Only relevant climbs the ladder
payload.note: DISCONTINUITY → INTERPRET → MEASURE → COMPARE TO CRITERIA → DISPOSITION

beats.narration: When in doubt, doubt resolves toward relevant. Reprocess, or use one permitted wipe-and-redevelop. Believe what returns — then sign only what you can defend.

payload.sub: Believe what returns — sign only what you can defend.
